text: Activation of Strong C–H Bonds Using Synthetic Iron(IV)-Oxo Complexes: A Unified Heterogeneous/Metal-Organic Framework Approach Enzymatic heme and non-heme high-valent iron-oxo species readily activate strong C–H bonds, however, synthesizing or generating molecular catalysts that can achieve this level of reactivity toward C–H bonds has proven highly challenging, although due in part to the terminal iron-oxo being highly reactive and therefore short lived. It is additionally true that the previous reported examples of active synthetic iron(IV)-oxo precursors lack the necessary high-spin S = 2 ground state seen in non-heme iron(IV)-oxo enzymes. Here we report that the metal-organic framework (MOF) Fe 2 (dobdc) and its magnesium-diluted analogue, Fe 0.1 Mg 1.9 (dobdc), both catalyze the transformation of ethane into ethanol and selectively acetaldehyde when oxidized with N 2 O. Analysis of the activation energy, and Mo\+2 analyses indicate that the catalyst active species is provided by the MOF itself. Electronic structure calculations for isolated metal sites within the framework predict that a high-spin S = 2 iron(IV)-oxo would be the active oxidizing species. 1 Department of Chemistry, University of California, Berkeley, California 94720-1460, USA 2 The Molecular Foundry, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Berkeley, California 94720- 1460, USA 3 Center for Neutron Research, National Institute of Standards and Technology, Gaithersburg, Maryland 20899, USA 4 Department of Chemistry, Chemical Theory Center, and Supercomputing Institute, University of Minnesota, Minneapolis, Minnesota, 55455-0431, USA 5 Department of Chemistry, NIS and INSTM Reference Centres University of Turin, Via Quarello 15, I-10135 Torino, Italy 6 Physical Biosciences Division, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Berkeley, California 94720-1460, USA 7 Department of Chemical Engineering, University of Delaware, Newark, Delaware 19716, USA 8 Materials Sciences Division, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Berkeley, California 94720, USA
